mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical 2.0 percent annualized trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.5 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to discount future corporate cash flows at a higher rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5 percent, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings recessions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects rapid supply-side normalization that alleviates inflationary bottlenecks, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns in more than 40 percent of recovery episodes, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate sustained consumer confidence and credit availability, which directly supports discretionary corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth exhibits a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across three consecutive business cycles, or the predictive R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in retail and food services sales exceeding 5 percent triggers a downward revision in quarterly earnings estimates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional momentum effect completely reverses the predictive direction within a 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When rising consumer price inflation coincides with an elevated federal funds rate, real interest rates turn deeply positive, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In early recessionary phases, a month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate acts as a leading indicator of imminent earnings contractions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Unemployment increases correlate positively with 5-day forward returns in more than 45 percent of identified recession onset periods, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below zero over a 5-year walk-forward test.
